gloss.en: things built or arranged from parts

words.example: The bridge and other structures were very old.

gloss.en: people who study at a school

words.example: All the students passed the exam.
She studied hard for the test.
His studies focus on ancient history.
They are studying for their exams.

gloss.en: ways of doing or making things

words.example: The shop sells many styles of shoes.

gloss.en: a small group within a committee

words.example: A subcommittee will review the new plan.
The metal was subjected to high heat.
Math and science are my favorite subjects.
He submitted his report on time.

gloss.en: a smaller part of a section

words.example: Read the rules in the next subsection.

gloss.en: a company owned by a larger one

words.example: The bank owns a small subsidiary abroad.
Some substances can be harmful to skin.

gloss.en: used something in place of another

words.example: She substituted butter with oil in the recipe.
They succeeded in finishing the project.
He suffered from a bad cold.
She suggested we leave early.
He kept suggesting new ideas.
Thank you for your helpful suggestions.
The report suggests a new approach.

gloss.en: was right or good for something

words.example: The quiet town suited her well.
Large sums of money were spent.
The supervisors checked our work.

gloss.en: added to complete or improve something

words.example: The book has supplementary notes at the back.
The store supplied all the food.
We bought supplies for the trip.
Her family supported her dream.
They are supporting the new plan.
This wall supports the whole roof.

gloss.en: expected or meant to do something

words.example: You were supposed to call me.
See the note cited supra in chapter two.

gloss.en: the outer or top parts of things

words.example: Clean all the kitchen surfaces well.
Tall trees surrounded the house.

gloss.en: sets of questions to gather opinions

words.example: We sent surveys to all customers.
Susan works at the library.
The police suspected he was lying.

gloss.en: stopped for a time, or hung up

words.example: The game was suspended due to rain.

gloss.en: kept up or maintained over time

words.example: They sustained their effort for years.
Sweden is known for its cold winters.
Switzerland has many tall mountains.
Sydney has a famous opera house.
Math uses many special symbols.

gloss.en: a meeting to discuss a topic

words.example: Experts spoke at the science symposium.
A cough and fever are common symptoms.
This jacket is made of synthetic cloth.

gloss.en: sets of parts that work together

words.example: The school uses two computer systems.

gloss.en: pieces of furniture with flat tops

words.example: We set up tables for the party.
The books were taken from the shelf.
It takes an hour to get there.
She is taking the children to school.
We talked for hours last night.
They were talking on the phone.
The two sides held peace talks.

gloss.en: large containers for liquid or gas

words.example: The water tanks were nearly empty.
The team met its sales targets.

gloss.en: a tax on goods coming in

words.example: The government raised the tariff on steel.

gloss.en: pieces of work to be done

words.example: She has many tasks to finish today.
My mother taught me to cook.
Only part of the income is taxable.
The new law changed business taxation.
We pay taxes every year.
Taylor lives next door to us.
Our teachers are very kind.
Both teams played very well.

gloss.en: drops of water from the eyes

words.example: Her eyes filled with tears.
He works for a big tech company.
The chef uses old cooking techniques.
New technologies change our lives fast.

gloss.en: the hard white parts in the mouth

words.example: Brush your teeth before bed.

gloss.en: an old way to send messages by wire

words.example: Long ago people used the telegraph.
She is telling us a story.
He always tells the truth.

gloss.en: measures of how hot or cold

words.example: Winter temperatures dropped below zero.
The story has a clear temporal order.
He tended to arrive late.
She tends to speak quietly.

gloss.en: a state in the United States

words.example: Tennessee is famous for its music.
This is my tenth visit here.
The termination of the contract was sudden.

gloss.en: relating to land or an area

words.example: The two nations had a territorial dispute.
The empire ruled many territories.
Her success is a testament to hard work.

gloss.en: checked something to see if it works

words.example: They tested the new machine yesterday.
The witness testified before the judge.
We have two tests next week.

gloss.en: a large state in the United States

words.example: Texas is a very big state.
The town has a large textile factory.
She read several old texts.
I will always love thee.

gloss.en: a rule in math proven true

words.example: We learned a new math theorem today.
Scientists tested many theories.
Music can have a therapeutic effect.
Read the rules and obey them therein.
The house and the value thereof were noted.
The land and the house built thereon were sold.
The contract and any changes thereto must be signed.
The walls have good thermal protection.
I have many things to do today.
He thinks the plan is good.

gloss.en: parts when something is divided by three

words.example: Two thirds of the class agreed.
Thomas is my older brother.
Mr. Thompson teaches our science class.
Thou art my dearest friend.
Please share your thoughts with us.
Thousands of people came to the show.
The storm threatened the small town.
He threw the ball to me.
The ball was thrown over the fence.
She thrust the door open.
He tied his shoes quickly.
The two families have close ties.

gloss.en: a word ending that forms nouns

words.example: Words like 'action' end with tion.

gloss.en: soft paper, or body cell groups

words.example: She used tissues to dry her eyes.

gloss.en: names of books, films, or jobs

words.example: I forgot the titles of those books.
Tokyo is a very busy city.
She told me the good news.

gloss.en: large units of weight; very much

words.example: The truck carried tons of sand.
He took my hand and smiled.
He keeps his tools in a box.
We discussed many topics in class.
